title: cotoneaster tomentosus
intro: Cotoneaster tomentosus, also known as the woolly cotoneaster, is a deciduous shrub native to the Himalayas and Tibet, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and clusters of small white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, where its berries, leaves, and roots are valued for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic effects. The health benefits of Cotoneaster tomentosus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and lower blood pressure. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations can also be taken in the form of a tincture or syrup, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential interactions with certain medications.
